title: Curd Rice (Daddhojanam)
description: Temple-style curd rice made with soft, lightly mashed rice blended with thick curd and milk, finished with a fragrant ghee tempering of mustard seeds, cashews, ginger, curry leaves, and a pinch of hing. Garnished with pomegranate for a cooling, divine finish.

preparation.instruction: Cook rice until very soft and mushy.
tips: Temple-style daddhojanam uses extra-soft, slightly overcooked rice. Use a 1:3 rice-to-water ratio instead of the usual 1:2 for the right mushy texture.
Sona Masoori or any short-grain rice works best. Avoid basmati — it stays too firm.
If using a pressure cooker, cook for 4–5 whistles on medium heat.
subSteps: Wash 1.5 cups of sona masoori rice 2–3 times until water runs clear.
Add washed rice to a pressure cooker with 4 cups of water.
Cook on medium heat for 4–5 whistles until rice is very soft and slightly mushy.
Let pressure release naturally. Open and give a gentle stir — rice should mash easily.
Spread cooked rice in a wide bowl and let it cool to room temperature (about 10–15 minutes). Do not mix with curd while hot.

tips: Fry cashews first before the mustard seeds so they turn golden-brown without burning.
Hing (asafoetida) is a signature temple touch — even a small pinch makes a noticeable difference.
subSteps: Grate or finely mince ginger.
Slit green chillies lengthwise (remove seeds for milder heat).
Break dried red chillies in half.
Wash and pat dry curry leaves — wet curry leaves splatter in hot oil.
Measure out mustard seeds, cumin seeds, cashews, and hing.
Deseed pomegranate and keep seeds ready for garnish.

ingredients.name: Milk
ingredients.notes: full-fat; balances sourness and makes it creamy

ingredients.name: Hing (Asafoetida)
ingredients.notes: a generous pinch; the temple secret

ingredients.name: Curry Leaves
ingredients.notes: pat dry before adding to hot oil

ingredients.name: Cashews
ingredients.notes: halved or whole; fried until golden

ingredients.name: Fresh Coconut
ingredients.notes: grated; optional but adds temple-style sweetness

ingredients.name: Pomegranate Seeds
ingredients.notes: for garnish; adds sweetness and color

steps.instruction: Cool the cooked rice and mash lightly.
tips: Mashing the rice partially helps it absorb the curd evenly. Do not blend it smooth — some texture is important.
subSteps: Once rice has cooled to room temperature, transfer to a large mixing bowl.
Using the back of a spoon or your hand, lightly mash the rice — not completely smooth, just enough to break up the grains.
The texture should resemble a loose porridge, not a paste.

steps.instruction: Mix rice with curd, milk, and salt.
tips: Adding milk alongside curd is the temple trick — it makes the rice creamy and prevents it from becoming too sour as it sits.
Always add curd to room-temperature or cool rice, never to hot rice. Hot rice will curdle the curd and make it watery.
Taste the mixture before tempering and adjust salt. The curd rice should taste mildly salty — the tempering will add more flavor.
subSteps: Add 2.5 cups thick curd to the mashed rice.
Pour in 0.5 cup milk.
Add 1.5 tsp salt.
Mix everything together until the rice is evenly coated and the consistency is soft and creamy.
If the mixture feels too thick, add a splash more milk. It should be loose — it will firm up as it sits.
If using grated fresh coconut, fold it in now.
Taste and adjust salt. Set aside.

tips: Keep the heat on medium — ghee burns faster than oil. The entire tadka takes under 2 minutes once the ghee is hot.
Cashews go in first as they need the longest to turn golden. Add mustard seeds only after cashews are light golden.
The hing should sizzle immediately when added. If it does not, the ghee is not hot enough.
subSteps: Heat 2 tsp ghee in a small pan or tadka ladle over medium heat.
Add cashews and fry, stirring, until light golden — about 60 seconds. Remove and set aside (or leave in pan).
In the same ghee, add mustard seeds. Let them splutter and pop completely.
Add cumin seeds — they will sizzle immediately.
Add broken dried red chillies and fry for 10 seconds until they darken slightly.
Add slit green chillies and grated ginger. Stir for 15–20 seconds.
Add hing. It will sizzle and release a sharp aroma — stir once.
Add curry leaves last — stand back as they splatter. Fry for 5–10 seconds until crisp.
Turn off the heat.

steps.instruction: Pour the tempering over the curd rice and mix gently.
tips: Pour the entire hot tadka directly over the curd rice — the sizzle blooms the aromas into the rice instantly.
Mix gently with a light hand. Over-mixing after the tadka will break up the fragrant whole spices.
subSteps: Pour the hot ghee tadka (with all the spices and cashews) directly over the curd rice mixture.
Hear the sizzle — this is normal and correct.
Gently fold the tempering into the curd rice, mixing just enough to distribute it evenly.
Taste once more and adjust salt if needed.

tips: Pomegranate is not just garnish — its slight sweetness and burst of juice complement the tang of the curd rice beautifully.
Curd rice tastes best at room temperature or slightly chilled. Avoid serving it cold straight from the fridge — the rice stiffens.
Serve with a simple pickle (mango or lime achaar) on the side for an authentic temple meal experience.
subSteps: Transfer curd rice to a serving bowl.
Scatter pomegranate seeds generously on top.
Serve immediately at room temperature, or refrigerate for up to 2 hours and bring back to room temperature before serving.
Pairs best with mango pickle, lime pickle, or papad.

seo.metaTitle: Temple-Style Curd Rice (Daddhojanam) — Cooling South Indian Classic
seo.metaDescription: Make authentic temple-style curd rice (Daddhojanam) at home with soft rice, thick curd, milk, ghee tempering, and pomegranate. A cooling, divine South Indian recipe.
